title: Quest Diagnostics Incorporated (DGX) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Quest Diagnostics (DGX) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A modest 4% growth rate reflects the mature nature of the clinical lab industry, offset by continuous reimbursement pressures, with growth relying heavily on tuck-in acquisitions.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A lower 7.5% discount rate is appropriate given Quest's highly predictable, recession-resistant cash flows and defensive business model.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.0% terminal growth rate aligns with long-term inflation and population growth, reflecting a steady but slow-growth mature market.

thesisParagraphs: Quest Diagnostics operates as a fundamental pillar of the U.S. healthcare system. Along with LabCorp, it commands a formidable duopoly in the independent clinical laboratory space. Its primary competitive advantage is scale: massive, automated testing facilities and dense logistical networks allow Quest to perform routine tests at a fraction of the cost of hospital-based labs.

While pricing pressures from Medicare and private insurers present a perpetual headwind, Quest counters this through sheer volume and an aggressive strategy of acquiring hospital outreach labs. It is a slow-growth, defensive business that reliably generates over $1 billion in free cash flow annually, making it a stable asset trading near its fair intrinsic value.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini project only 4% growth for Quest?
faq.answer: The core routine testing market is mature and faces constant reimbursement cuts. Growth must be manufactured through market share gains and acquisitions rather than organic volume explosions.

faq.question: Why is the discount rate so low at 7.5%?
faq.answer: Quest provides essential healthcare services, making its cash flows highly resilient even during economic downturns, which lowers its overall risk profile.

faq.question: What is Quest's main competitive advantage?
faq.answer: Cost advantage derived from immense scale. Its centralized labs and dense courier networks result in the lowest per-unit cost for diagnostic testing in the industry.
